findings.claim: CLINUVEL is a commercial-stage rare-disease/specialty-pharma company whose revenue is primarily from SCENESSE® sales.
findings.source_title: A S X A N N O U N C E M E N T
findings.notes: Company release snippet states FY2025 revenue and commercialization details; used as the primary positioning source.

findings.claim: CLINUVEL reported FY2025 profit before tax of A$51.6 million and EBIT of about A$52 million with a 54% margin; EBITDA was not explicitly surfaced in the accessible text.

data.ebitda: not clearly disclosed in retrieved text

findings.source_title: A S X A N N O U N C E M E N T
findings.notes: The task requested operating metrics; EBIT is a useful proxy here, but EBITDA could not be cleanly extracted from the PDF text available to the tool.

findings.claim: A practical close peer set for CLINUVEL includes commercial rare-disease / specialty-pharma listed names, with broader biotech references kept separate.
findings.source_title: Top Specialty Pharma Leaders Reshaping the Rare Disease Market
findings.notes: This is a secondary industry article; useful for peer identification only, not for financial facts. I separated more profitable specialty-pharma names and broader references from the closer commercial rare-disease set.

findings.claim: BioMarin is a much larger commercial rare-disease peer, with 2025 revenue of about US$2.9 billion and strong operating margin commentary.

data.margin_comment: non-GAAP operating margin expected ~40% in 2026 (excluding Amicus transaction impact)

findings.source_title: BioMarin Reports Fourth Quarter and Full-year 2025 Financial and Operating Results
findings.notes: Market-cap source was available from a search snippet; revenue/margin details came from the company press release search snippet. Geography was discussed qualitatively rather than in a simple segment table.

findings.claim: BioMarin’s market capitalization was about US$10.71 billion on CompaniesMarketCap.
findings.notes: Market-cap figure came from a search snippet rather than a fetched page, so treat as approximate current point-in-time data.

findings.claim: Catalyst Pharmaceuticals is a profitable commercial rare-disease specialty-pharma peer, with market cap around US$3.17 billion.
findings.notes: Market-cap figure came from a search snippet. Catalyst is especially relevant as a profitable commercial rare-disease comp.

findings.claim: Alnylam is a much larger commercial rare-disease peer; 2025 annual report data show revenues of US$3.714 billion, net product revenues of US$2.987 billion, royalty revenue of US$174.0 million, and net income of US$313.7 million.
findings.notes: Useful as an upper-end commercial rare-disease reference; revenue scale is far above CLINUVEL and its valuation multiples may not be directly comparable.

findings.claim: CLINUVEL’s market capitalization was shown as about US$0.33 billion in April 2026.
findings.notes: Search-snippet market-cap estimate only; use as indicative scale, not audited fact.

not_found: A clean, company-reported FY2025 EBITDA figure for CLINUVEL could not be cleanly extracted from the retrieved PDF text.

Reliable, directly fetched current EV / sales, EV / EBITDA, and P/E values for each peer were not available within the source budget; available data were mostly market-cap snippets and company revenue/margin disclosures.
A simple, authoritative peer-ranking table from a single source was not located; peer set was assembled from company-commercial profile plus secondary industry references.
summary: CLINUVEL is best framed as a profitable commercial-stage rare-disease/specialty-pharma company rather than a typical development-stage biotech. The closest listed comp bucket is other commercial rare-disease names such as BioMarin, Catalyst, Sarepta, PTC, Travere, Krystal, Alnylam, Ascendis, and Zevra; broader or less directly comparable references include Ultragenyx, Takeda, and Recordati. Current market-cap snippets suggest CLINUVEL is much smaller than BioMarin/Alnylam and closer to the small-/mid-cap rare-disease specialty-pharma universe.
